modules.module_title: Introduction to Sports Nutrition, Macronutrients & Micronutrients
Energy Metabolism, Caloric Requirements & Hydration Strategies
Dietary Supplements, Ergogenic Aids & Safety in Sports
Body Composition Management: Weight Loss, Muscle Gain & Eating Disorders
Professional Ethics, Diet Planning Basics & Client Counseling

questions.question: Sports Nutrition & Diet Basics program ka primary objective kya hai?

options.A: Athletes ko dynamic performance energy check, calorie plans, hydration aur recovery nutrition guidelines seekhana
options.B: Operating chemical manufacturing machines in factories
options.C: Writing codes for banking database security locks
options.D: Zero dietary adjustments planning

questions.explanation: Sports nutrition energy demand mapping, glycogen reload cycles, body composition management aur safe supplement usage guidelines par work karta hai.

questions.question: Nutrition science me 'Proteins' body cells ke liye kya primary function provide karte hain?

options.A: Muscle tissue repair, synthesis aur growth post resistance workouts
options.B: Providing instant sweetness sugars to brain cells
options.C: Storing body fats in subcutaneous tissue layers
options.D: Proteins have zero physiological role

questions.explanation: Protein amino acids chains se form hota hai jo workout micro-tears cells ko heal aur hypertrophy synthesize karta hai.

questions.question: Hydration checks guidelines me, workout timing active play session me sweat water loss check?

options.A: Weigh athlete before and after workout (every 1 kg weight loss equals approx 1 to 1.5 liters fluid deficit)
options.B: Measure height differences before and after workout
options.C: Weight changes do not relate to sweat losses
options.D: Zero weight measurements exist

questions.explanation: Pre and post weight measurement accurate fluid loss checks confirm karta hai, guiding custom electrolyte reloading.

questions.question: Pre-workout meal planning checks me carb intake standard timing and type guideline?

options.A: Complex carbs (eg oats, brown rice) 2 to 3 hours before workout (steady glucose release during session)
options.B: Heavy oily cheese burger 10 minutes before training start
options.D: Pre-workout food timing has zero effect

questions.explanation: Complex carbs slowly digest hote hain, maintaining stable blood sugar loops during intensive training peaks.

questions.question: Sports supplements checks me 'Creatine Monohydrate' consumption ka main physiological benefit?

options.A: Raises muscle phosphocreatine stores, boosting explosive short-power ATP generation (eg sprinting, lifts)
options.B: Reducing calcium absorption in bone structures
options.D: Creatine does not participate in energy loops

questions.explanation: Creatine ATP-CP cycle speed up karti hai, letting athletes lift heavier and sprint faster with faster recovery.

questions.question: Sports diet planning parameters checks me 'BMR' (Basal Metabolic Rate) kya represent karta hai?

options.A: Calories body requires to maintain basic life survival functions at complete rest
options.B: Total calories burned during heavy weight lifting sets only
options.C: Speed of breathing runs per minute
options.D: Zero calorie significance

questions.explanation: BMR baseline organs functionality energy expenditure check measure karta hai (eg breathing, heart beat energy costs).

questions.question: Sports diet strategies me 'Carbohydrate Loading' method kis type of sports event me use kiya jata hai?

options.A: Endurance events lasting more than 90 minutes (eg marathon running, long-distance cycling)
options.B: Short 100m sprint runs lasting 10 seconds
options.D: Carb loading is forbidden in sports

questions.explanation: Carb loading muscle glycogen stores saturate karti hai, postponing performance drop (hitting the wall) during endurance tasks.

questions.question: Sports nutrition safety checks me 'WADA' code compliance ka primary objective kya hai?

options.A: World Anti-Doping Agency rules check run karna, identifying and banning illegal performance enhancing substances
options.B: Calculating company monthly sales taxes
options.D: WADA rules do not apply to diet plans

questions.explanation: WADA compliance ensures fair play and protects athlete health from dangerous banned drugs and hormones.

questions.question: Body weight management plans me 'Caloric Deficit' parameter kis target objective me setup kiya jata hai?

options.A: Fat loss / Weight reduction (consuming fewer calories than body expends daily)
options.B: Rapid muscle mass bulking setups
options.D: Caloric deficit has zero weight impact

questions.explanation: Caloric deficit body energy demands meet karne ke liye fat tissue oxidation triggers set karta hai.

questions.question: Sports nutrition counseling ethics guidelines me, trainer role boundary lines?

options.A: Provide macro guides and dietary recommendations, but refer pathology diagnosis and medical illnesses to clinical dietitians
options.B: Write prescription drugs for thyroid and diabetes management directly
options.C: Guarantee clients that supplements will cure all health issues
options.D: Zero boundaries exist between coaches and doctors

questions.explanation: Nutrition coaches advise on lifestyle and performance diets. Clinical pathology/illness diagnostics fall under registered medical practitioners.

questions.question: Nutrition checks 'Micronutrients' what is primary classification?

options.A: Vitamins and Minerals (required in small quantities for metabolic enzymes and cell functions)
options.B: Proteins and carbohydrates
options.D: Micronutrients do not exist in nutrition

questions.explanation: Vitamins (organic) and minerals (inorganic) act as key cofactors in energy pathways and bone health.

questions.question: Energy metabolism checks 'Aerobic Lipolysis' matches which intensity zone?

options.A: Low-to-moderate intensity long duration workouts (using fats as primary fuel source with oxygen)
options.B: Explosive 100m sprint runs under 10 seconds
options.D: Zero energy systems match

questions.explanation: Aerobic fat burning dominates in lower intensity ranges, where oxygen supplies keep pace with demand.

questions.question: Post-workout nutrition 'The Anabolic Window' what is the target nutrient mix?

options.A: Fast-digesting proteins and simple carbohydrates within 30-45 minutes post-workout
options.B: High fat meals and green tea only
options.C: Pure water with zero calorie additions
options.D: Post-workout meals are skipped to keep fat low

questions.explanation: Protein helps repair micro-tears, while carbs replenish depleted glycogen stores after workouts.

questions.question: Dietary supplements 'Whey Protein Isolate' how does it differ from Whey Concentrate?

options.A: Isolate is filtered further to remove maximum lactose and fat, containing higher protein concentration per scoop
options.B: Isolate contains raw unprocessed milk sugars
options.C: Isolate has lower absorption rates than concentrate
options.D: Zero differences exist

questions.explanation: Isolates undergo processing to strip fats and lactose, yielding 90%+ protein content, making them ideal for lactose-sensitive athletes.

questions.question: Sports nutrition logs 'Electrolyte replacement' what is the role of sodium in hydration?

options.A: Maintains fluid balance, osmotic pressure, and prevents hyponatremia (water intoxication)
options.B: Decreases blood volume during cardio work
options.D: Sodium has zero role in hydration logs

questions.explanation: Sodium is lost in sweat. Replacing it prevents cramping and maintains fluid balance.

options.B: Rapid muscle mass bulking setups
options.D: Caloric deficit has zero weight impact

options.B: Decreases blood volume during cardio work
options.D: Sodium has zero role in hydration logs

questions.explanation: Sodium is lost in sweat. Replacing it prevents cramping and maintains fluid balance.

questions.question: Sports nutrition basics 'Carbohydrates classification' what are complex carbohydrates?

options.A: Polysaccharides containing long chemical chains (eg oatmeal, sweet potatoes, whole wheat)
options.B: Monosaccharides like glucose and fructose (simple sugars)
options.D: Complex carbs are not digestible

questions.explanation: Complex carbs (polysaccharides) digest slowly, providing sustained energy for long workouts.

questions.question: Energy systems checks 'Lactic Acid System' (Anaerobic Glycolysis) what is primary byproduct?

options.A: Lactate and hydrogen ions (causes muscle burn sensation during intense 1-2 minute activities)
options.B: Oxygen molecules
options.D: Carbon dioxide only

questions.explanation: Anaerobic glycolysis breaks down glucose without oxygen, yielding lactate and hydrogen ions, which cause temporary muscle burn.

questions.question: Pre-workout nutrition 'Caffeine timing' when should caffeine be consumed for performance gains?

options.A: 30 to 60 minutes before workout (allows caffeine blood levels to peak)
options.B: Immediately as workout ends
options.D: Caffeine has zero impact on performance

questions.explanation: Caffeine takes 30-60 mins to reach peak concentration, boosting focus and performance when taken beforehand.

questions.question: Ergogenic aids 'Beta-Alanine' what side effect is commonly felt after intake?

options.A: Paresthesia (harmless tingling sensation in skin/hands/face)
options.B: Severe drowsiness and sleep states
options.D: Beta-Alanine has zero side effects

questions.explanation: Beta-alanine can cause paresthesia, a tingling sensation. It is harmless and can be managed with smaller doses.

questions.question: Body composition management 'Eating Disorders' what characterizes Bulimia Nervosa?

options.A: Cycles of binge eating followed by purging behaviors (eg self-induced vomiting, excessive exercise)
options.B: Strict self-starvation leading to severe weight loss (Anorexia)
options.C: Obsession with eating only clean organic foods (Orthorexia)
options.D: Zero eating disorders exist in sports

questions.explanation: Bulimia nervosa features cycles of binging and purging, which can cause electrolyte imbalances in athletes.

questions.question: Complex carbohydrates polysaccharides sweet potato?

options.A: Polysaccharides containing long chemical chains (eg oatmeal, sweet potatoes, whole wheat)
options.B: Monosaccharides like glucose and fructose (simple sugars)
options.D: Complex carbs are not digestible

questions.explanation: Complex carbs (polysaccharides) digest slowly, providing sustained energy for long workouts.

questions.explanation: Beta-alanine can cause paresthesia, a tingling sensation. It is harmless and can be managed with smaller doses.

options.D: Zero eating disorders exist in sports

questions.explanation: Bulimia nervosa features cycles of binging and purging, which can cause electrolyte imbalances in athletes.

questions.question: Sports nutrition basics 'Fats classification' what are essential fatty acids?

options.A: Omega-3 and Omega-6 fatty acids (body cannot produce them; must be obtained from diet)
options.B: Saturated fats found in processed meats
options.C: Trans fats found in bakery items
options.D: Essential fats do not exist in nutrition

questions.explanation: Omega-3 and Omega-6 are essential fats. They support cell structure and reduce inflammation.

questions.question: Energy systems checks 'Aerobic System' (Oxidative Phosphorylation) what fuels are utilized?

options.A: Carbohydrates and Fats with oxygen (primary fuel for activities lasting over 3 minutes)
options.B: Phosphocreatine stores without oxygen
options.D: Oxidative system does not use calories

questions.explanation: The aerobic system uses oxygen to burn carbs and fats, providing steady fuel for long activities.

questions.question: Intra-workout nutrition 'Long endurance events' what hydration mix is recommended?

options.A: Isotonic sports drink containing 6-8% carbohydrates and sodium (speeds up gastric emptying)
options.B: Ice-cold carbonated soda drinks
options.C: Pure concentrated fruit juice without water
options.D: Intra-workout drinks are strictly avoided

questions.explanation: A 6-8% carb and sodium mix (isotonic) digests quickly, restoring fuel and electrolytes during long events.

questions.question: Supplements evaluation 'Banned substances' what is risk of buying uncertified supplements?

options.A: Cross-contamination with banned steroids/stimulants at manufacturing facilities, leading to doping fails
options.B: Higher sugar content in standard protein powders
options.C: Product losing its nutritional value quickly
options.D: No risks exist when buying supplements

questions.explanation: Uncertified supplements can contain trace steroids, leading to failed drug tests. Look for 'Informed-Sport' tags.

questions.question: Body composition management 'Female Athlete Triad' what are the three conditions?

options.A: Low energy availability, menstrual dysfunction (amenorrhea), and low bone mineral density (osteoporosis)
options.B: High protein intake, high muscle mass, and high speed
options.C: High calorie diet, obesity, and diabetes
options.D: Triad does not apply to athletes

questions.explanation: The Triad involves low energy, missed periods, and bone loss. It is a serious condition requiring medical care.

questions.question: Essential fatty acids Omega 3 Omega 6?

options.A: Omega-3 and Omega-6 fatty acids (body cannot produce them; must be obtained from diet)
options.B: Saturated fats found in processed meats
options.C: Trans fats found in bakery items
options.D: Essential fats do not exist in nutrition

questions.explanation: Omega-3 and Omega-6 are essential fats. They support cell structure and reduce inflammation.

options.A: Carbohydrates and Fats with oxygen (primary fuel for activities lasting over 3 minutes)
options.B: Phosphocreatine stores without oxygen
options.D: Oxidative system does not use calories

questions.explanation: The aerobic system uses oxygen to burn carbs and fats, providing steady fuel for long activities.

options.D: Intra-workout drinks are strictly avoided

questions.explanation: A 6-8% carb and sodium mix (isotonic) digests quickly, restoring fuel and electrolytes during long events.

options.A: Cross-contamination with banned steroids/stimulants at manufacturing facilities, leading to doping fails
options.B: Higher sugar content in standard protein powders
options.C: Product losing its nutritional value quickly
options.D: No risks exist when buying supplements

questions.explanation: Uncertified supplements can contain trace steroids, leading to failed drug tests. Look for 'Informed-Sport' tags.

questions.question: Female Athlete Triad amenorrhea osteoporosis?

options.A: Low energy availability, menstrual dysfunction (amenorrhea), and low bone mineral density (osteoporosis)
options.B: High protein intake, high muscle mass, and high speed
options.C: High calorie diet, obesity, and diabetes
options.D: Triad does not apply to athletes

questions.explanation: The Triad involves low energy, missed periods, and bone loss. It is a serious condition requiring medical care.

questions.question: Sports nutrition basics 'Water-soluble vitamins' which vitamins belong to this group?

options.A: Vitamin C and Vitamin B-Complex (not stored in body, excess excreted in urine)
options.B: Vitamins A, D, E, and K (fat-soluble)
options.D: Water-soluble vitamins do not exist

questions.explanation: Vitamins B and C are water-soluble. They must be consumed daily as they are not stored in body tissues.

questions.question: Energy balance 'TDEE' what does it stand for in diet planning?

options.A: Total Daily Energy Expenditure (BMR + physical activity + TEF energy costs)
options.B: Total Diet Efficiency Evaluation
options.D: TDEE has zero calculation role

questions.explanation: TDEE is the total calories you burn daily, calculated by adding your BMR, activity level, and digestion energy costs.

questions.question: Pre-workout meal 'Optimal fiber content' what is standard recommendation?

options.A: Keep fiber low in pre-workout meals (fiber slows digestion and can cause stomach cramps during runs)
options.B: Consume high-fiber bran flakes 10 minutes before workouts
options.C: Fiber is strictly avoided in all diets
options.D: Fiber content does not affect workout digestion

questions.explanation: Low fiber and fat in pre-workout meals prevent stomach distress, ensuring quick energy absorption.

questions.question: Sports supplements 'Glutamine' what is its primary function in recovery?

options.A: Supports immune system function and gut health during periods of intense training
options.B: Increases muscle glycogen stores rapidly
options.C: Acts as a primary fat burner in cells
options.D: Glutamine has zero health benefits

questions.explanation: Glutamine is an amino acid that supports gut health and immune function during heavy training blocks.

questions.question: Body composition management 'Anorexia Nervosa' what is key symptom?

options.A: Severe restriction of food intake leading to dangerously low body weight and fear of gaining weight
options.B: Eating large meals followed by purging
options.D: Anorexia has zero symptoms

questions.explanation: Anorexia nervosa involves strict calorie limits and a distorted body image, requiring clinical care.

questions.question: Water soluble Vitamin C B complex?

options.A: Vitamin C and Vitamin B-Complex (not stored in body, excess excreted in urine)
options.B: Vitamins A, D, E, and K (fat-soluble)
options.D: Water-soluble vitamins do not exist

questions.explanation: Vitamins B and C are water-soluble. They must be consumed daily as they are not stored in body tissues.

questions.question: TDEE Total Daily Energy Expenditure BMR?

options.A: Total Daily Energy Expenditure (BMR + physical activity + TEF energy costs)
options.B: Total Diet Efficiency Evaluation
options.D: TDEE has zero calculation role

questions.explanation: TDEE is the total calories you burn daily, calculated by adding your BMR, activity level, and digestion energy costs.

questions.question: Low fiber pre workout cramps?

options.A: Keep fiber low in pre-workout meals (fiber slows digestion and can cause stomach cramps during runs)
options.B: Consume high-fiber bran flakes 10 minutes before workouts
options.C: Fiber is strictly avoided in all diets
options.D: Fiber content does not affect workout digestion

questions.explanation: Low fiber and fat in pre-workout meals prevent stomach distress, ensuring quick energy absorption.

questions.question: Glutamine immune system gut recovery?

options.A: Supports immune system function and gut health during periods of intense training
options.B: Increases muscle glycogen stores rapidly
options.C: Acts as a primary fat burner in cells
options.D: Glutamine has zero health benefits

questions.explanation: Glutamine is an amino acid that supports gut health and immune function during heavy training blocks.

questions.question: Anorexia Nervosa low body weight fear?

options.A: Severe restriction of food intake leading to dangerously low body weight and fear of gaining weight
options.B: Eating large meals followed by purging
options.D: Anorexia has zero symptoms

questions.explanation: Anorexia nervosa involves strict calorie limits and a distorted body image, requiring clinical care.

questions.question: Sports nutrition basics 'Fat-soluble vitamins' which vitamins are stored in body fats?

options.A: Vitamins A, D, E, and K (can cause toxicity if taken in extreme doses)
options.B: Vitamin C and B-Complex
options.D: Fat-soluble vitamins do not exist

questions.explanation: Vitamins A, D, E, and K are stored in fat tissues. High doses can build up and cause toxicity.

questions.question: Diet planning 'TEF' what does TEF represent in calorie calculations?

options.A: Thermic Effect of Food (energy cost of digesting and processing nutrients; protein has highest TEF)
options.B: Total Energy Fitness
options.D: TEF has zero calculation role

questions.explanation: TEF is the energy needed to digest food. Protein requires the most energy (20-30% of its calories) to process.

questions.question: Pre-workout hydration 'Timeline' what volume of water is recommended 2-4 hours before sports?

options.A: 5 to 7 ml of water per kilogram of body weight (approx 350-500 ml for a 70kg athlete)
options.B: 2 liters of water 10 minutes before
options.C: Avoid drinking water before match starts
options.D: Pre-workout hydration has no timeline rules

questions.explanation: Drinking water 2-4 hours before allows the kidneys to balance hydration levels before the match.

questions.question: Sports supplements 'Caffeine doses' what is safe, effective performance dose range?

options.A: 3 to 6 mg of caffeine per kilogram of body weight (avoid exceeding 9 mg/kg)
options.B: 20 mg per kg daily
options.D: Caffeine is not dosed by body weight

questions.explanation: A dose of 3-6 mg/kg boosts performance without causing jitters or heart rate spikes.

questions.question: Body composition management 'Weight loss rates' what is safe weekly fat loss target?

options.A: 0.5% to 1% of body weight per week (helps preserve lean muscle mass)
options.B: 5 kg of fat loss daily
options.D: Weight loss speed has zero safety limits

questions.explanation: A target of 0.5-1% body weight loss weekly reduces fat while preserving muscle mass.

questions.question: Fat soluble vitamins A D E K?

options.A: Vitamins A, D, E, and K (can cause toxicity if taken in extreme doses)
options.B: Vitamin C and B-Complex
options.D: Fat-soluble vitamins do not exist

questions.explanation: Vitamins A, D, E, and K are stored in fat tissues. High doses can build up and cause toxicity.

questions.question: TEF Thermic Effect of Food digestion protein?

options.A: Thermic Effect of Food (energy cost of digesting and processing nutrients; protein has highest TEF)
options.B: Total Energy Fitness
options.D: TEF has zero calculation role

questions.explanation: TEF is the energy needed to digest food. Protein requires the most energy (20-30% of its calories) to process.

questions.question: Pre workout hydration 5ml per kg?

options.A: 5 to 7 ml of water per kilogram of body weight (approx 350-500 ml for a 70kg athlete)
options.B: 2 liters of water 10 minutes before
options.C: Avoid drinking water before match starts
options.D: Pre-workout hydration has no timeline rules

questions.explanation: Drinking water 2-4 hours before allows the kidneys to balance hydration levels before the match.

questions.question: Caffeine dose 3 to 6 mg per kg?

options.A: 3 to 6 mg of caffeine per kilogram of body weight (avoid exceeding 9 mg/kg)
options.B: 20 mg per kg daily
options.D: Caffeine is not dosed by body weight

questions.explanation: A dose of 3-6 mg/kg boosts performance without causing jitters or heart rate spikes.

questions.question: Safe weekly weight loss 0.5 to 1 percent?

options.A: 0.5% to 1% of body weight per week (helps preserve lean muscle mass)
options.B: 5 kg of fat loss daily
options.D: Weight loss speed has zero safety limits

questions.explanation: A target of 0.5-1% body weight loss weekly reduces fat while preserving muscle mass.

questions.question: Sports nutrition basics 'Protein requirements' what is daily target for heavy resistance athletes?

options.A: 1.6 to 2.2 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight (essential to rebuild micro-tears)
options.B: 0.8 grams per kg daily (sedentary standard)
options.D: Resistance athletes do not require extra protein

questions.explanation: Strength athletes need 1.6-2.2 g/kg of protein daily to support muscle repair and hypertrophy.

questions.question: Intra-workout hydration 'Sweat rates' why is sodium replenishment critical for long tennis matches?

options.A: Prevents muscle cramps and heat exhaustion by maintaining extracellular fluid volumes
options.B: Speeds up fat oxidation rates
options.D: Sodium has zero hydration value on court

questions.explanation: Replacing sodium lost in sweat prevents heat cramps and maintains fluid balance during long matches.

questions.question: Dietary supplements 'Beta-Alanine' how does it delay muscle fatigue?

options.A: Acts as precursor to carnosine, buffering hydrogen ions and acid accumulation in muscle cells
options.B: Increases oxygen transport capacity in blood
options.C: Acts as instant glucose sugar replacement
options.D: Beta-Alanine does not affect fatigue

questions.explanation: Beta-alanine raises muscle carnosine levels, buffering hydrogen ions to delay fatigue during intense exercises.

questions.question: Body composition management 'Relative Energy Deficiency in Sport' (REDs) what is the cause?

options.A: Chronic energy intake deficit relative to exercise energy expenditure (low energy availability)
options.B: Too much carbohydrate loading in diets
options.D: REDs has zero relation to caloric balance

questions.explanation: REDs occurs when energy intake is too low for exercise demands, affecting hormone health and metabolic rate.

questions.question: Sports nutrition counseling 'Diet plan design' what is first step when meeting client?

options.A: Collect dietary history, assess body composition, define goals, and screen for eating disorders
options.B: Prescribe fat burner supplements immediately
options.C: Write a 1200 kcal low-carb diet on spot
options.D: No assessments are needed before plan design

questions.explanation: Assessing history and screening for disorders ensures you design a safe, effective diet plan.

questions.question: Strength athletes protein 1.6 to 2.2 grams?

options.A: 1.6 to 2.2 grams of protein per kilogram of body weight (essential to rebuild micro-tears)
options.B: 0.8 grams per kg daily (sedentary standard)
options.D: Resistance athletes do not require extra protein

questions.explanation: Strength athletes need 1.6-2.2 g/kg of protein daily to support muscle repair and hypertrophy.

questions.question: Sodium sweat replacement extracellular fluid?

options.A: Prevents muscle cramps and heat exhaustion by maintaining extracellular fluid volumes
options.B: Speeds up fat oxidation rates
options.D: Sodium has zero hydration value on court

questions.explanation: Replacing sodium lost in sweat prevents heat cramps and maintains fluid balance during long matches.

questions.question: Beta Alanine carnosine hydrogen buffer fatigue?

options.A: Acts as precursor to carnosine, buffering hydrogen ions and acid accumulation in muscle cells
options.B: Increases oxygen transport capacity in blood
options.C: Acts as instant glucose sugar replacement
options.D: Beta-Alanine does not affect fatigue

questions.explanation: Beta-alanine raises muscle carnosine levels, buffering hydrogen ions to delay fatigue during intense exercises.

questions.question: REDs Relative Energy Deficiency Sport cause?

options.A: Chronic energy intake deficit relative to exercise energy expenditure (low energy availability)
options.B: Too much carbohydrate loading in diets
options.D: REDs has zero relation to caloric balance

questions.explanation: REDs occurs when energy intake is too low for exercise demands, affecting hormone health and metabolic rate.

questions.question: Diet history goals eating disorder screening?

options.A: Collect dietary history, assess body composition, define goals, and screen for eating disorders
options.B: Prescribe fat burner supplements immediately
options.C: Write a 1200 kcal low-carb diet on spot
options.D: No assessments are needed before plan design

questions.explanation: Assessing history and screening for disorders ensures you design a safe, effective diet plan.

questions.question: Sports nutrition 'Dehydration check' what is standard indicator in field settings?

options.A: Urine color chart (pale yellow/straw color shows good hydration; dark amber shows dehydration)
options.B: Body temperature checks
options.D: Hydration cannot be checked in field

questions.explanation: A urine color chart is a simple, effective tool for athletes to monitor hydration in the field.

questions.question: Diet planning 'Daily protein distribution' what is best frequency for muscle protein synthesis?

options.A: 0.3 to 0.4 grams of protein per kg body weight spaced every 3 to 4 hours across day
options.B: Consuming entire daily protein in one single evening meal
options.C: Spacing protein intake to once every 12 hours
options.D: Protein spacing does not affect muscle gains

questions.explanation: Spacing protein (approx 20-40g per meal) every 3-4 hours maximizes muscle protein synthesis throughout the day.

questions.question: Supplements evaluation 'Omega-3 fatty acids' what is primary benefit for athletes?

options.A: Reduces muscle inflammation, supports heart health, and aids joint recovery
options.B: Acts as instant glucose sugar replacement
options.D: Omega-3 has zero athletic value

questions.explanation: Omega-3s (EPA/DHA) reduce muscle soreness and support joint health, aiding recovery.

questions.question: Sports nutrition counseling 'Scope of Practice' what is boundary limit when client seeks thyroid medication?

options.A: Politely state that prescribing medications exceeds your scope, and refer client to endocrinologist
options.B: Prescribe iodine supplements and thyroid hormones yourself
options.C: Change diet macros to treat thyroid issues without medical referrals
options.D: Coaches can prescribe thyroid drugs legally

questions.explanation: Prescribing medication is a medical act. Refer clients with hormone issues to licensed specialists.

questions.question: Sports nutrition 'Sports drinks types' what is hypotonic drink?

options.A: Fluid concentration lower than blood (speeds up water absorption; best for fast hydration without carb energy)
options.B: Fluid concentration higher than blood (hypertonic)
options.D: Hypotonic drinks do not exist

questions.explanation: Hypotonic drinks have a lower concentration than blood, allowing rapid water absorption for fast hydration.

questions.question: Athlete sweat rate pre post weight?

options.B: Measure height differences before and after workout
options.C: Weight changes do not relate to sweat losses
options.D: Zero weight measurements exist

questions.question: Pre-workout meal complex carbs oats?

options.B: Heavy oily cheese burger 10 minutes before training start
options.D: Pre-workout food timing has zero effect

questions.explanation: Complex carbs slowly digest hote hain, maintaining stable blood sugar loops during intensive training peaks.

questions.question: Creatine Monohydrate ATP CP pathway?

options.A: Raises muscle phosphocreatine stores, boosting explosive short-power ATP generation (eg sprinting, lifts)
options.B: Reducing calcium absorption in bone structures
options.D: Creatine does not participate in energy loops

questions.explanation: Creatine ATP-CP cycle speed up karti hai, letting athletes lift heavier and sprint faster with faster recovery.

questions.question: BMR baseline calorie resting metabolic?

options.A: Calories body requires to maintain basic life survival functions at complete rest
options.B: Total calories burned during heavy weight lifting sets only
options.C: Speed of breathing runs per minute
options.D: Zero calorie significance

questions.explanation: BMR baseline organs functionality energy expenditure check measure karta hai (eg breathing, heart beat energy costs).

questions.question: Carbo loading glycogen saturation endurance?

options.A: Endurance events lasting more than 90 minutes (eg marathon running, long-distance cycling)
options.B: Short 100m sprint runs lasting 10 seconds
options.D: Carb loading is forbidden in sports

questions.explanation: Carb loading muscle glycogen stores saturate karti hai, postponing performance drop (hitting the wall) during endurance tasks.
